Delivered reactive seed-search tooling and default-on peel/dense fixup path. Current validated result: [2048,32000] fuzz 0 failures, mean overhead near documented floor with known rare-tail/high-N residuals split to wirehair-0yk; long-term full table regeneration remains wirehair-jwg.

Ran 1000 paired baseline/v2 random-loss experiments for each block-byte parameter set: 1280, 102400, and 1048576 bytes, each with independent seed streams. All cohorts completed with no decode failures; results reported extra-symbol distribution and approximate per-message processing time.

Added a tuned-profile compare option and ran fixed-N baseline vs v2_tuned sweeps. Found the differentiator: base v2 is seed-equivalent to baseline, while tuned v2 changes overhead distributions. Tuned v2 improved some bands (e.g. N=12000/bb=1280: 0.030 -> 0.010 mean OH; capped 1MiB: 0.050 -> 0.000) but worsened others (e.g. N=12000/capped 100KiB: 0.020 -> 0.060; N=320/bb=1280: 0.014 -> 0.026). Throughput remained mostly within noise or slightly worse for tuned v2 on large payloads.

Implemented and tested two optimization-loop tools in codec/: compare --v2-profile auto with a conservative auto-min-delta threshold to avoid rare-event overfitting, and densecount to sweep dense row count deltas with paired seeds. Experiments found no universal peel/tuned-seed win; tuned seed calibration overfits unless thresholded. Dense count is a real tradeoff knob: reducing dense rows can improve create/decode throughput (e.g. N=320/100KiB dense -16 and N=12000/1280 dense -8), but candidates were loss-regime dependent and did not produce a universally safe overhead win. N=3200 dense -16 failed, establishing a safety bound.

Added densegrid paired dense-count/dense-seed sweep mode and compare-mode dense overrides. Repeated code-reading and smoke validation found no robust dense-count/seed candidate suitable for default policy: densegrid produced local candidates, but direct compare validation under the baseline-v2 random-loss protocol regressed or only traded overhead for slower decode. Production seed policy left unchanged.
